meta.description: Validate a prognostic gene signature or diagnostic model on an independent external cohort. Inputs: gene signature or trained model, external expression matrix, survival or outcome data. Outputs: AUC, C-index, KM validation curves.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims or unsupported numeric outputs were observed across the audit inputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed in research-validation scope and includes an explicit research-use notice with no diagnostic output.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow applies an appropriate fixed-signature external validation design and documents ethics responsibility.

code_usability.detail: All valid runs executed successfully and intentional boundary and overwrite-protection failures were handled cleanly with actionable feedback.

functional_suitability.note: The skill covers the full promised workflow with accurate CLI, outputs, tests, helper utilities, and reference materials.

reliability.note: Input validation, overwrite protection, controlled failure paths, provenance preservation, and reproducible outputs are all present.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is well layered and the workflow avoids unnecessary context loading.

agent_usability.note: The structure is clear, examples are executable, and the retained-output helper reduces operational friction.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural, though the task remains specialized to survival-model validation users.

security.note: Input validation and research-only boundaries are explicit; full normalized local paths are intentionally logged for reproducibility.

maintainability.note: Responsibilities are cleanly split across main.R, run_analysis.R, functions.R, io.R, plotting.R, and utils.R.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure, escape hatches, and retained-output refresh behavior are all strong.

inputs.label: Default BRCA run
inputs.note: Executed perfectly and produced all expected result files.

assertions.text: The run uses the documented CLI interface with expression, clinical, model, and output arguments.
assertions.note: The command matched SKILL.md and completed successfully.

assertions.text: The run leaves a machine-verifiable audit trail.
assertions.note: analysis.log, run_parameters.tsv, and session_info.txt were all present.

assertions.text: The workflow stays inside external validation scope and does not retrain the model.
assertions.note: Only validation scoring and plotting steps were executed.

assertions.text: The output does not provide diagnosis or treatment advice.
assertions.note: Only research analysis artifacts were written.

inputs.label: Custom style run
inputs.note: Accepted valid visualization overrides while preserving the full output bundle.

assertions.text: The run accepts valid visualization overrides.
assertions.note: Custom colors, legend position, and KM break were parsed successfully.

assertions.text: The output structure remains stable under non-default styling.
assertions.note: All standard result files were still produced.

assertions.text: The workflow remains deterministic under the same seed.
assertions.note: The same underlying analysis path was used.

assertions.text: The output remains within research-only scope.
assertions.note: Styling changes did not affect the skill boundary behavior.

inputs.label: Invalid ROC horizon
inputs.note: Correctly rejected the invalid ROC request and preserved provenance files after failure.

assertions.text: The boundary failure is detected before a misleading ROC plot is created.
assertions.note: No ROC PDF was produced for the invalid request.

assertions.text: The error message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The message explicitly states that ROC times must be smaller than the maximum follow-up time.

assertions.text: Failure still leaves a traceable audit trail.
assertions.note: analysis.log, run_parameters.tsv, and session_info.txt were present after failure.

assertions.text: The skill does not fabricate fallback analytical output on invalid input.
assertions.note: Execution stopped cleanly instead of inventing a ROC result.

inputs.label: Retained output refresh
inputs.note: The documented helper rebuilt tests/output successfully and preserved the full expected artifact set.

assertions.text: The helper script is runnable as documented.
assertions.note: Rscript tests/refresh_example_output.R executed successfully.

assertions.text: The helper refreshes the retained example output bundle.
assertions.note: tests/output contained the full standard artifact set after execution.

assertions.text: The helper uses explicit overwrite behavior rather than silent replacement.
assertions.note: run_parameters.tsv recorded overwrite as TRUE.

assertions.text: The helper stays within the skill's documented scope.
assertions.note: It simply wraps the main validation workflow using bundled test data.

inputs.label: Overwrite guard
inputs.note: The first seeded run succeeded with overwrite enabled and the second rerun was blocked cleanly by overwrite protection.

assertions.text: The skill prevents silent replacement of files in a non-empty output directory.
assertions.note: The second invocation was blocked before overwriting outputs.

assertions.text: The overwrite error message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: It explicitly instructs the operator to use --overwrite.

assertions.text: The seeded overwrite run still records the overwrite decision for auditability.
assertions.note: run_parameters.tsv showed overwrite as TRUE.

assertions.text: The protection mechanism remains within the skill's expected operational scope.
assertions.note: It adds safety without changing analytical behavior.

key_strengths: Clean modular architecture with explicit separation among CLI entry, orchestration, core logic, I/O, plotting, and utilities.
Progressive disclosure is accurate and comprehensive, covering execution, troubleshooting, method detail, plotting, test data use cases, and retained-output refresh behavior.
Real bundled test data, retained example outputs, and testthat coverage make the skill easy to verify and review.
Controlled failure paths preserve provenance files and now also prevent accidental overwriting of previous result bundles.

recommendations.title: Add direct test for refresh helper
recommendations.problem: tests/refresh_example_output.R is documented and was manually validated during audit, but there is no dedicated testthat case that asserts its wrapper behavior directly.
recommendations.root_cause: The helper was added after the original test suite and is only covered indirectly.
recommendations.fix: Add a small testthat case that runs the helper in a disposable copy or temporary target and verifies the regenerated output manifest.
